stage_prompt.name: Copy Platform Tools to devplatform (Parallel Run)

comprehension_gate.rule: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION. Present structured summary. STOP and WAIT for Lead Architect approval.

vikunja_access_protocol.rationale: Stage 1 Anomaly A5 -- Executor surfaces vary in MCP-tool exposure; REST is documented as functionally equivalent fallback
vikunja_access_protocol.intro: Stage operations that read or write Vikunja MAY use either Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools OR direct Representational State Transfer (REST) against the Vikunja API. Both paths are byte-equivalent at the Vikunja server. Preference: MCP when the Executor surface exposes mcp__vikunja__* tools; REST is the documented fallback when MCP is not surfaced.
vikunja_access_protocol.preferred: Tool prefixes: mcp__vikunja__list_projects, mcp__vikunja__create_project, mcp__vikunja__list_tasks, mcp__vikunja__create_task, mcp__vikunja__update_task, mcp__vikunja__add_task_comment, mcp__vikunja__list_labels, mcp__vikunja__add_label_to_task. Precondition: Vikunja v2.3.0 server running on http://localhost:3456 (start via Start-Process C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\vikunja\vikunja-v2.3.0-windows-4.0-amd64.exe; poll /api/v1/info before first call).
vikunja_access_protocol.fallback: Direct REST via Invoke-RestMethod. Authentication flow: $cred = Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json $pass = $cred.mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS # read-only; do NOT write back $body = @{ username='blarai'; password=$pass } | ConvertTo-Json $token = (Invoke-RestMethod -Uri 'http://localhost:3456/api/v1/login' -Method POST ` -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json').token $headers = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" } # Subsequent calls: GET/PUT/POST against /api/v1/projects, /api/v1/tasks, # /api/v1/labels, etc.
vikunja_access_protocol.credential_source: Single canonical source for Vikunja credentials: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json key path mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS. Both MCP path and REST path read this same value. The User-scoped VIKUNJA_PASS env var (persisted at Stage 0.0.5) is used by the MCP server process itself; for direct PowerShell scripts, read from the .vscode/mcp.json file as shown above.
vikunja_access_protocol.safety: Neither path satisfies Master Plan section D.1 enforcement -- D.1 is enforced by tools/_vikunja_client.py (created in Stage 2.1) for fleet RUNTIME tools, not for stage-execution operations. Stage operations using either path must still pass explicit project_id when the operation is project-scoped.

v2_updates.note: Additive to v1 work_items 3.1–3.10. Apply alongside.

item.name: Expand tool-copy list with v2 additions
item.description: v1 item 3.1 copies 7 directories. v2 baseline adds these specific files to the same destination tree (devplatform/tools/...). Recursive directory copy already covers them — verify explicitly.

additional_copies.copy_tests: Any test_blarai_next_task_resolver*.py, test_diff_builder*.py, test_active_tasks*.py under tools/tests/ — copy to devplatform/tools/tests/.

item.verify: Compare LOC totals: BlarAI source vs devplatform copy must match within 0 lines (no truncation).

item.name: Expand doc-copy patterns with governance and wake templates
item.description: v1 item 3.2 misses governance docs (~2377 LOC across 7 files) and wake templates (6 cron-fired wake prompts).

item.name: Capture v2 LOC totals in copy manifest
item.description: For audit, record LOC counts of every v2-added file in `devplatform/.copy_manifest_v2.yaml` so Stage 5 cleanup can verify nothing was orphaned.

stage_prompt.objective:
COPY (not move) all platform tools from `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\` to `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\`. Install dependencies in devplatform's .venv. Verify the devplatform copy runs independently. Do NOT delete originals yet. Do NOT switch any external surfaces (MCP configs, scheduled tasks) — those are Stage 4.
After this stage, both BlarAI/tools/ and devplatform/tools/ contain full working copies of the platform. BlarAI copy remains the active one (MCP configs still point to it; scheduled tasks still disabled).

preconditions.check: Stage 2 complete — multi-project seam merged into BlarAI tools.

item.name: Copy platform tool directories

do_not_copy.item: tools/tests/ — copy ONLY the vikunja/fleet/autonomy tests; skip BlarAI-specific tests.

item.name: Copy platform doctrine docs to devplatform
item.description: Copy (not move) the platform-layer documentation files.

item.name: Copy claude_cowork and claude_projects (if populated)
Populate devplatform pyproject.toml with platform dependencies
item.description: Expand the placeholder from Stage 1.7 with real dependencies (read from current BlarAI pyproject.toml, keep only platform-tool deps).
item.pattern: Copy the relevant `[project.dependencies]` and `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` entries from BlarAI/pyproject.toml, filtering to only what platform tools need (requests, pyyaml, pytest, etc.). BlarAI-runtime deps (openvino, transformers, etc.) stay out.

item.name: Copy MCP config templates
item.description: Copy `.mcp.json` and `.vscode/mcp.json` to devplatform AND update paths inside them to reference devplatform locations. Critically, the devplatform copies pick up `tools/vikunja_mcp/` from `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\` not from BlarAI.

secret_handling.warning: VIKUNJA_PASS is currently plaintext in both MCP configs (F-1 finding). For the devplatform copies, reference an env var instead: `"VIKUNJA_PASS": "${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}"`. Lead Architect sets the env var in the new devplatform workspace before Stage 4.

item.name: Smoke test devplatform fleet tool (parallel with BlarAI copy still active)
item.preflight: $BLARAI_PID = (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.platform\vikunja_project_ids.yaml | Select-String 'blarai_project_id:\s*(\d+)').Matches.Groups[1].Value if (-not $BLARAI_PID) { throw 'BLARAI_PID not found in registry; complete Stage 1.4 first.' }
item.expected: Runs to completion from devplatform. Same output as running from BlarAI copy.
item.important: Do NOT let it write any state (use --dry-run or equivalent). Both copies can't write simultaneously yet.

item.name: Copy BlarAI docs that are BlarAI-only to ensure they stay
item.description: Verification check — no action needed, but confirm we did NOT copy BlarAI-only docs (ADRs, Task4.*, P5_*, ledgers, IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, TEST_GOVERNANCE.md) to devplatform.
item.expected: Empty (none of those should be in devplatform).

item.name: Commit devplatform state
item.command: cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git add .; git commit -m "Stage 3: copy platform tools and doctrine from BlarAI"

item.name: Update STATUS.md
item.description: Mark Stage 3 COMPLETE. Record: devplatform commit hash, list of directories copied, smoke test result.

rollback.trigger: Dependencies fail to install, or devplatform smoke test fails.

steps.step: cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git reset --hard HEAD~1 (first commit from Stage 1 stays)
Delete devplatform/tools and devplatform/docs contents, recreate empty skeleton.
BlarAI is unchanged — rollback here has zero BlarAI impact.

exit_criteria.criterion: All 7 platform tool directories copied to devplatform/tools/.
devplatform .venv has all platform dependencies installed.
Platform doctrine docs copied to devplatform/docs/.
Smoke test passes from devplatform copy.
BlarAI tools/ and docs/ unchanged (BlarAI copy still authoritative).
MCP configs (system-wide) still point to BlarAI paths — not yet repointed.
